input:
The behavior tree represents a robotic system that continuously repeats a sequence to pursue goals. Within this sequence, it first attempts to pick up an item if it sees one (condition: SeeItem), and if it fails to find an item, it proceeds to place the item it is holding (condition: HoldingItem). This process continues indefinitely due to the num_cycles="-1" attribute in the Repeat node.

Additionally, there's an initialization behavior tree (Init) which simply prints "Initialize" when executed. The tree also includes various actions and conditions such as AlwaysRunning, GetItemPose, Trigger, and Print with specific functionalities.

In summary, the overall task of the behavior tree is to create a loop where the robot repeatedly attempts to pick up items it sees, and if unsuccessful, it places the item it is holding. It also includes an initialization step to print a message at the start.
output:
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="-1">
            <Sequence name="Pursue Goals">
                <Fallback name="No Item to pick">
                    <Inverter name="Not">
                        <Condition ID="SeeItem" name="See an Item"/>
                    </Inverter>
                    <Action ID="Pick" name="Pick it up"/>
                </Fallback>
                <Fallback name="No Item in gripper">
                    <Inverter name="Not">
                        <Condition ID="HoldingItem" name="Holding an Item"/>
                    </Inverter>
                    <Action ID="Place" name="Place it"/>
                </Fallback>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Init">
        <Action ID="Print" message="Initialize"/>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="AlwaysRunning"/>
        <Action ID="GetItemPose"/>
        <Condition ID="HoldingItem"/>
        <SubTree ID="Init"/>
        <Action ID="Pick"/>
        <Action ID="Place"/>
        <Action ID="Print">
            <input_port name="message"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="SeeItem"/>
        <Action ID="Trigger">
            <input_port name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>

input: The behavior tree outlines a robotic task sequence. First, it checks the battery level, then opens the gripper, proceeds to approach an object, and finally closes the gripper. This indicates a series of actions that a robot will perform in a specific order, ensuring that the battery is sufficient before attempting any physical interactions with objects.
output:
<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree" >
     <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Sequence name="root_sequence">
            <CheckBattery   name="check_battery"/>
            <OpenGripper    name="open_gripper"/>
            <ApproachObject name="approach_object"/>
            <CloseGripper   name="close_gripper"/>
        </Sequence>
     </BehaviorTree>
 </root>

input:
The behavior tree orchestrates the decision-making process for a virtual character. The character's main priorities are addressing its needs (hunger, thirst, and sleep) and performing various job-related tasks.

When the character is hungry, it will first attempt to find food and then eat. If thirsty, it will search for a drink and then consume it. When sleepy, it will locate a bed and proceed to sleep. These actions are prioritized within the "Hunger," "Thirst," and "Sleepy" sub-trees.

In addition to managing its basic needs, the character also engages in job-related activities. It follows a sequence of steps for standard jobs, including initializing the job, claiming items, finding tools, moving, equipping tools, working, and finishing the job. It also handles specific tasks such as leaving the map or trading at a market stall.

The overarching "MainTree" coordinates these activities, ensuring that the character addresses its needs before attending to its job responsibilities. If
output:
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Hunger">
        <SequenceStar name="Hunger">
            <FallbackStar>
                <Sequence>
                    <Condition ID="IsHungry"/>
                    <Action ID="FindFood"/>
                </Sequence>
            </FallbackStar>
            <Action ID="Move"/>
            <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
            <ForceSuccess>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="FindDining"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                </SequenceStar>
            </ForceSuccess>
            <Action ID="Eat"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobStandard">
        <SequenceStar name="StandardJobs">
            <Action ID="InitJob"/>
            <Action ID="ClaimItems"/>
            <Action ID="FindTool"/>
            <Action ID="Move"/>
            <Action ID="EquipTool"/>
            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="20">
                <Inverter>
                    <SequenceStar>
                        <Inverter>
                            <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
                        </Inverter>
                        <Action ID="Move"/>
                        <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                        <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                        <Action ID="Move"/>
                        <Action ID="DropItem"/>
                    </SequenceStar>
                </Inverter>
            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
            <Action ID="GetWorkPosition"/>
            <Action ID="Move"/>
            <Action ID="Work"/>
            <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Jobs">
        <FallbackStar>
            <SequenceStar name="Leave">
                <Condition ID="IsTimeToLeave"/>
                <Action ID="GetExitPosition"/>
                <Action ID="Move"/>
                <Action ID="LeaveMap"/>
            </SequenceStar>
            <SequenceStar name="WorkAtMarketStall">
                <Action ID="GetMarketStallPosition"/>
                <Action ID="Move"/>
                <Action ID="Trade"/>
            </SequenceStar>
        </FallbackStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <FallbackStar name="GnomeTrader">
            <FallbackStar name="Needs">
                <SubTree ID="Hunger"/>
                <SubTree ID="Thirst"/>
                <SubTree ID="Sleepy"/>
            </FallbackStar>
            <SubTree ID="Jobs"/>
        </FallbackStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Sleepy">
        <SequenceStar name="Sleep">
            <Condition ID="IsSleepy"/>
            <ForceSuccess>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="FindBed"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                </SequenceStar>
            </ForceSuccess>
            <Action ID="Sleep"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Thirst">
        <SequenceStar name="Thirst">
            <FallbackStar>
                <Sequence>
                    <Condition ID="IsThirsty"/>
                    <Action ID="FindDrink"/>
                </Sequence>
            </FallbackStar>
            <Action ID="Move"/>
            <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
            <ForceSuccess>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="FindDining"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                </SequenceStar>
            </ForceSuccess>
            <Action ID="Drink"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
        <Action ID="ButcherAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="ClaimItems"/>
        <Action ID="Drink"/>
        <Action ID="DropItem"/>
        <Action ID="Eat"/>
        <Action ID="EquipTool"/>
        <Action ID="FindBed"/>
        <Action ID="FindDrink"/>
        <Action ID="FindFood"/>
        <Action ID="FindDining"/>
        <Action ID="FindTool"/>
        <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
        <Action ID="GetExitPosition"/>
        <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
        <Action ID="GetJob"/>
        <Action ID="GetMarketStallPosition"/>
        <Action ID="GetWorkPosition"/>
        <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="HarvestAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="Hunger"/>
        <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
        <Action ID="InitButcherJob"/>
        <Action ID="InitJob"/>
        <Condition ID="IsButcherJob"/>
        <Condition ID="IsHungry"/>
        <Condition ID="IsSleepy"/>
        <Condition ID="IsThirsty"/>
        <Condition ID="IsTimeToLeave"/>
        <Condition ID="IsVeryHungry"/>
        <Condition ID="IsVeryThirsty"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobStandard"/>
        <SubTree ID="Jobs"/>
        <Action ID="LeaveMap"/>
        <Action ID="Move"/>
        <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
        <Action ID="RandomMove"/>
        <Action ID="ReleaseAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="Sleep"/>
        <SubTree ID="Sleepy"/>
        <SubTree ID="Thirst"/>
        <Action ID="Trade"/>
        <Action ID="Work"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
</root>

input:
The behavior tree orchestrates the actions of a character in a simulated environment. The character is programmed to perform various tasks based on its needs and job assignments. It includes behaviors such as combat, obtaining a uniform, addressing hunger and thirst, performing specific jobs like butchering animals or hauling items, sleeping, training, and supervising training.

The character's behavior is governed by conditions such as being in combat, hungry, thirsty, sleepy, or assigned to a particular job type. For each task, there are sequences of actions to be executed, including finding specific locations, moving, picking up or dropping items, and completing the assigned job. Additionally, the character can abort a job if necessary.

Overall, the behavior tree outlines a comprehensive set of behaviors for the character, ensuring that it responds to its needs and fulfills its designated responsibilities within the simulated environment.
output:
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Combat">
        <SequenceStar>
            <Condition ID="IsInCombat"/>
            <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            <ForceSuccess>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="GetTarget"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Sequence>
                        <Condition ID="TargetAdjacent"/>
                        <Action ID="AttackTarget"/>
                    </Sequence>
                </SequenceStar>
            </ForceSuccess>
            <Condition ID="IsInCombat"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="GetUniform">
        <SequenceStar name="GetUniform">
            <Action ID="CheckUniform"/>
            <Fallback>
                <Action ID="Move"/>
                <Action ID="UniformCleanUp"/>
            </Fallback>
            <Action ID="EquipUniform"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Hunger">
        <SequenceStar>
            <Condition ID="IsHungry"/>
            <Sequence>
                <SequenceStar name="Hunger">
                    <Action ID="FindFood"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                    <ForceSuccess>
                        <SequenceStar>
                            <Action ID="FindDining"/>
                            <Action ID="Move"/>
                        </SequenceStar>
                    </ForceSuccess>
                    <Action ID="Eat"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Condition ID="IsFull"/>
            </Sequence>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobButcherAnimal">
        <BB_Precondition expected="ButcherAnimal" key="JobType">
            <Fallback>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="FinalMoveAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="ButcherAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobDyeAnimal">
        <BB_Precondition expected="DyeAnimal" key="JobType">
            <Fallback>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="InitJob"/>
                    <Action ID="ClaimItems"/>
                    <SubTree ID="MoveAllItemsToJob"/>
                    <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="FinalMoveAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="DyeAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobHarvestAnimal">
        <BB_Precondition expected="HarvestAnimal" key="JobType">
            <Fallback>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="HarvestAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="ReleaseAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobHaulMultiple">
        <BB_Precondition expected="HauleMultipleItems" key="JobType">
            <Fallback>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="InitJob"/>
                    <Action ID="FindTool"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="EquipTool"/>
                    <Inverter>
                        <Sequence>
                            <Inverter>
                                <Condition ID="AllPickedUp"/>
                            </Inverter>
                            <Sequence>
                                <Action ID="Move"/>
                                <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                            </Sequence>
                            <Action ID="ReturnAlwaysRunning"/>
                        </Sequence>
                    </Inverter>
                    <Condition ID="AllPickedUp"/>
                    <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="DropAllItems"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobLeadAnimalToPasture">
        <BB_Precondition expected="LeadAnimalToPasture" key="JobType">
            <Fallback>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="FinalMoveAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="ReleaseAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobStandard">
        <Fallback>
            <SequenceStar name="StandardJobs">
                <Action ID="InitJob"/>
                <Action ID="ClaimItems"/>
                <Action ID="FindTool"/>
                <Action ID="Move"/>
                <Action ID="EquipTool"/>
                <SubTree ID="MoveAllItemsToJob"/>
                <Action ID="GetWorkPosition"/>
                <Action ID="Move"/>
                <Action ID="Work"/>
                <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
            </SequenceStar>
            <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobTameAnimal">
        <BB_Precondition expected="TameAnimal" key="JobType">
            <Fallback>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="TameAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="FinalMoveAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="ReleaseAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Jobs">
        <SequenceStar>
            <Action ID="GetJob"/>
            <FallbackStar>
                <SubTree ID="JobButcherAnimal"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobDyeAnimal"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobLeadAnimalToPasture"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobHarvestAnimal"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobTameAnimal"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobHaulMultiple"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobStandard"/>
            </FallbackStar>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <SubTree ID="Jobs"/>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="MoveAllItemsToJob">
        <Sequence>
            <Inverter>
                <Sequence>
                    <Inverter>
                        <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
                    </Inverter>
                    <SequenceStar>
                        <Action ID="Move"/>
                        <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                        <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                        <Action ID="Move"/>
                        <Action ID="DropItem"/>
                    </SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="ReturnAlwaysRunning"/>
                </Sequence>
            </Inverter>
            <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="OldMoveAllItemsToJob">
        <FallbackStar>
            <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="20">
                <Sequence>
                    <SequenceStar>
                        <Action ID="Move"/>
                        <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                        <Fallback>
                            <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                            <Action ID="DropItem"/>
                        </Fallback>
                        <Fallback>
                            <Action ID="Move"/>
                            <Action ID="DropItem"/>
                        </Fallback>
                        <Action ID="DropItem"/>
                    </SequenceStar>
                    <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
                </Sequence>
            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
            <ForceFailure>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </ForceFailure>
        </FallbackStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Sleepy">
        <SequenceStar name="Sleep">
            <Condition ID="IsSleepy"/>
            <ForceSuccess>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="FindBed"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                </SequenceStar>
            </ForceSuccess>
            <Action ID="Sleep"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Thirst">
        <SequenceStar>
            <Condition ID="IsThirsty"/>
            <Sequence>
                <SequenceStar name="Thirst">
                    <Action ID="FindDrink"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                    <ForceSuccess>
                        <SequenceStar>
                            <Action ID="FindDining"/>
                            <Action ID="Move"/>
                        </SequenceStar>
                    </ForceSuccess>
                    <Action ID="Drink"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Condition ID="IsDrinkFull"/>
            </Sequence>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Trainer">
        <SequenceStar name="Trainer">
            <Condition ID="IsTrainer"/>
            <Action ID="FindTrainerPosition"/>
            <Action ID="Move"/>
            <Action ID="SuperviseTraining"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Training">
        <SequenceStar name="Training">
            <Condition ID="IsTrainingTime"/>
            <Action ID="FindTrainingGround"/>
            <Action ID="Move"/>
            <Action ID="Train"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
        <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
        <Condition ID="AllPickedUp"/>
        <Action ID="AttackTarget"/>
        <Action ID="ButcherAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="CheckUniform"/>
        <Action ID="ClaimItems"/>
        <SubTree ID="Combat"/>
        <Action ID="Drink"/>
        <Action ID="DropAllItems"/>
        <Action ID="DropItem"/>
        <Action ID="DyeAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="Eat"/>
        <Action ID="EquipTool"/>
        <Action ID="EquipUniform"/>
        <Action ID="FinalMoveAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="FindBed"/>
        <Action ID="FindDining"/>
        <Action ID="FindDrink"/>
        <Action ID="FindFood"/>
        <Action ID="FindTool"/>
        <Action ID="FindTrainerPosition"/>
        <Action ID="FindTrainingGround"/>
        <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
        <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
        <Action ID="GetJob"/>
        <Action ID="GetTarget"/>
        <SubTree ID="GetUniform"/>
        <Action ID="GetWorkPosition"/>
        <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="HarvestAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="Hunger"/>
        <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
        <Action ID="InitButcherJob"/>
        <Action ID="InitJob"/>
        <Condition ID="IsButcherJob"/>
        <Condition ID="IsDrinkFull"/>
        <Condition ID="IsFull"/>
        <Condition ID="IsHungry"/>
        <Condition ID="IsInCombat"/>
        <Condition ID="IsSleepy"/>
        <Condition ID="IsThirsty"/>
        <Condition ID="IsTrainer"/>
        <Condition ID="IsTrainingTime"/>
        <Condition ID="IsVeryHungry"/>
        <Condition ID="IsVeryThirsty"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobButcherAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobDyeAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobHarvestAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobHaulMultiple"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobLeadAnimalToPasture"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobStandard"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobTameAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="Jobs"/>
        <Action ID="Move"/>
        <SubTree ID="MoveAllItemsToJob"/>
        <SubTree ID="OldMoveAllItemsToJob"/>
        <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
        <Action ID="RandomMove"/>
        <Action ID="ReleaseAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="ReturnAlwaysRunning"/>
        <Action ID="Sleep"/>
        <SubTree ID="Sleepy"/>
        <Action ID="SuperviseTraining"/>
        <Action ID="TameAnimal"/>
        <Condition ID="TargetAdjacent"/>
        <SubTree ID="Thirst"/>
        <Action ID="Train"/>
        <SubTree ID="Trainer"/>
        <SubTree ID="Training"/>
        <Action ID="UniformCleanUp"/>
        <Action ID="Work"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
</root>

input:
The behavior tree represents a decision-making process for an autonomous vehicle. The vehicle is programmed to perform various maneuvers based on environmental conditions and external commands.

The main behavior tree consists of multiple reactive sequences, each handling different driving scenarios such as pulling over, pulling out, side shifting, lane change, and avoidance. These sequences are executed based on the readiness and approval checks for each maneuver. If a specific condition is met, the corresponding action plan is executed.

Additionally, there are two sub-trees labeled "a" and "b", which handle specific aspects of the decision-making process related to lane changes and avoidance maneuvers. These sub-trees contain conditional checks and action plans for potential lane changes or avoidance actions based on approval and readiness criteria.

Overall, the behavior tree orchestrates the vehicle's decision-making process by evaluating conditions and executing appropriate action plans for safe and efficient navigation in dynamic environments.
output:
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Fallback>
            <ReactiveSequence>
                <Condition ID="PullOver_Request"/>
                <Condition ID="PullOver_Ready"/>
                <Action ID="PullOver_Plan" output="{output}"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
            <ReactiveSequence>
                <Condition ID="PullOut_Request"/>
                <Condition ID="PullOut_Ready"/>
                <Action ID="PullOut_Plan" output="{output}"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
            <ReactiveSequence>
                <Condition ID="SideShift_Request"/>
                <Condition ID="SideShift_Ready"/>
                <Action ID="SideShift_Plan" output="{output}"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
            <ReactiveSequence>
                <Condition ID="LaneChange_Request"/>
                <Fallback>
                    <Condition ID="LaneChange_Ready"/>
                    <Condition ID="ForceLaneChange_ForceApproval"/>
                </Fallback>
                <Action ID="LaneChange_Plan" output="{output}"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
            <ReactiveSequence>
                <Condition ID="Avoidance_Request"/>
                <Condition ID="Avoidance_Ready"/>
                <Action ID="Avoidance_Plan" output="{output}"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
            <Action ID="LaneFollowing_Plan" output="{output}"/>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="a">
        <ReactiveFallback>
            <Condition ID="LaneChange_CheckApproval"/>
            <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
                <Action ID="LaneChange_PlanCandidate" output="{output}"/>
            </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        </ReactiveFallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="b">
        <ReactiveFallback>
            <Condition ID="Avoidance_CheckApproval"/>
            <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
                <Action ID="Avoidance_PlanCandidate" output="{output}"/>
            </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        </ReactiveFallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Condition ID="Avoidance_CheckApproval"/>
        <Action ID="Avoidance_Plan">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="Avoidance_PlanCandidate">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="Avoidance_Ready"/>
        <Condition ID="Avoidance_Request"/>
        <Condition ID="ExternalApproval"/>
        <Condition ID="ForceApproval"/>
        <Condition ID="ForceLaneChange_ForceApproval"/>
        <Condition ID="LaneChange_CheckApproval"/>
        <Action ID="LaneChange_Plan">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="LaneChange_PlanCandidate">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LaneChange_Ready"/>
        <Condition ID="LaneChange_Request"/>
        <Action ID="LaneFollowing_Plan">
            <output_port name="output" type="boost::optional<autoware_planning_msgs::PathWithLaneId_<std::allocator<void> > >">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="LaneFollowing_PlanCandidate">
            <output_port name="output_candidate" type="boost::optional<autoware_planning_msgs::PathWithLaneId_<std::allocator<void> > >">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LaneFollowing_Ready"/>
        <Condition ID="LaneFollowing_Request"/>
        <Action ID="PullOut_Plan">
            <output_port name="output"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="PullOut_Ready"/>
        <Condition ID="PullOut_Request"/>
        <Action ID="PullOver_Plan">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="PullOver_Ready"/>
        <Condition ID="PullOver_Request"/>
        <Condition ID="SideShift_CheckApproval"/>
        <Action ID="SideShift_Plan">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SideShift_PlanCandidate">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="SideShift_Ready"/>
        <Condition ID="SideShift_Request"/>
        <SubTree ID="SubTree"/>
        <SubTree ID="a"/>
        <SubTree ID="b"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>

input:
The behavior tree is designed for a robot to navigate with periodic global path replanning and recovery actions. The main sequence "NavigateWithReplanning" ensures the robot follows a path at a rate of 1 Hz, recalculating the path if necessary. If the goal is reached, it proceeds to follow the path; otherwise, it computes a new path using "ComputePathToPose" and clears the global costmap before retrying. In case of recovery, it executes recovery actions such as clearing the local and global costmaps and spinning.

The RecoveryNode allows for a limited number of retries (6 in this case) before executing recovery actions. These actions include clearing the entire local and global costmaps and performing a spin maneuver.

Overall, the behavior tree enables the robot to navigate while handling potential failures by periodically replanning its path and executing recovery actions when needed.
output:
<!--
  This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
  recovery actions.
-->

<!-- <root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6">
      <Sequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <Fallback>
            <GoalReached/>
             <ComputePathToPose goal="${goal}" path="${path}"/>
          </Fallback>
        </RateController>
        <FollowPath path="${path}"/>
      </Sequence>
      <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
        <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="/local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="/global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
        <Spin/>
      </SequenceStar>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root> -->

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6">
      <Sequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <Fallback>
            <GoalReached/>
            <ComputePathToPose goal="${goal}" path="${path}"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="/global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </Fallback>
        </RateController>
        <FollowPath path="${path}"/>
      </Sequence>
      <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
        <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="/local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="/global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
      </SequenceStar>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>

<!-- <root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root> -->

input:
The behavior tree represents a decision-making process for an autonomous vehicle. The vehicle is programmed to continuously assess its surroundings and make driving decisions based on the information it gathers.

In the given tree:
- If the front of the ego lane is clear within 35.2 meters, the vehicle will continue in its current lane. Otherwise, it will attempt to bypass obstacles by checking the left lane's front and back, as well as the availability of driveable space in the left lane before executing a left bypass maneuver.
- If none of the conditions for bypassing are met, the vehicle defaults to staying in its current lane using the "ActionKeepLane" behavior.

Overall, the behavior tree enables the vehicle to navigate through traffic by prioritizing lane clearance and making necessary lane changes to ensure smooth and safe driving.
output:
<root main_tree_to_execute = "LatTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="LatTree">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <!-- lane : left, ego, right  -->
                <!-- distance : m -->
                <CheckLaneFrontClear which_lane="ego" distance="35.2" />
                <ActionKeepLane />
            </Sequence>

            <Sequence>
                <CheckLaneFrontClear which_lane="left" distance="35.2" />
                <CheckLaneBackClear which_lane="left" distance="20.2" />
                <CheckAvailDriveAreaClear which_lane="left" />
                <ActionLeftBypass />
            </Sequence>

            <ActionKeepLane />
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>

input:
The behavior tree outlines the actions for a robot or an autonomous agent. It begins with "go1" and then proceeds to execute "stay1" and "stay2" concurrently. After this, it continues with "go2" and "go3" in sequence. The tree also includes various other actions such as "dumpRun", "goClosestR", "isFull", "mineResources", "moveBaseToGoal", and "waitFrames", which can be utilized within the behavior tree.

In summary, the behavior tree orchestrates a series of actions for the robot, including movement ("go1", "go2", "go3"), resource management ("dumpRun", "isFull", "mineResources"), and task execution control ("stay1", "stay2", "waitFrames"). These actions are organized into sequences and can be further customized and expanded by incorporating additional actions from the provided TreeNodesModel.
output:
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Sequence name="Sequence1">
            <Action ID="go1"/>
            <Sequence name="Sequence2">
                <Action ID="stay1"/>
                <Action ID="stay2"/>
            </Sequence>
            <Action ID="go2"/>
            <Action ID="go3"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="dumpRun"/>
        <Action ID="go1"/>
        <Action ID="go2"/>
        <Action ID="go3"/>
        <Action ID="goClosestR"/>
        <Condition ID="isFull"/>
        <Action ID="mineResources"/>
        <Action ID="moveBaseToGoal">
            <inout_port name="key_name">goal</inout_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="stay1"/>
        <Action ID="stay2"/>
        <Action ID="waitFrames">
            <output_port name="barout"/>
            <inout_port name="baz"/>
            <input_port name="fooin"/>
            <input_port default="15" name="frames">frames</input_port>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>

input: The behavior tree consists of a single sequence that instructs the robot to move towards a specified goal. The goal is provided as an argument, allowing for flexibility in defining the target location for the robot to move to. This behavior tree is simple and direct, with the main task being to execute the "move" action within the defined sequence.

The behavior tree represents a robotic system that executes a sequence of tasks. Initially, it clears the local and global cost maps for navigation. Then, it sets a goal location (Goal_a) and computes a path to reach that goal using navigation algorithms. This entire sequence is repeated once. In summary, the robot's task involves preparing the environment for navigation by clearing cost maps, setting a specific goal location, and computing a path to navigate towards that goal.
output:
<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="1">
            <Sequence>
                <Nav2ClearLocalCostMap/>
                <Nav2ClearGlobalCostMap/>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_a" value="-0.7,-3.8" />
                <Nav2ComputePathClient goal="{Goal_a}" />
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>

input: The behavior tree outlines a sequence of tasks for a robot. First, it is instructed to navigate to the entrance, then ask a person, localize an unoccupied site, and finally navigate to the unoccupied site. The tasks are executed in sequence, with each subsequent task being activated only if the previous one has been successfully completed. This behavior tree essentially directs the robot through a series of steps aimed at reaching and interacting with an unoccupied site, likely as part of a larger operation or mission.
output:
<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree" >
     <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <ActivationSequence name="root_sequence">
            <NavigateToEntrance             name="navigate_to_entrance"/>
            <AskPerson                      name="ask_person"/>
            <LocalizeUnoccupiedSite         name="localize_unoccupied_site"/>
            <NavigateToUnoccupiedSite       name="navigate_to_unoccupied_site"/>
        </ActivationSequence>
     </BehaviorTree>
 </root>

input: The behavior tree orchestrates the decision-making process for a robot. It first checks various conditions such as stopping, battery status, diagnostic status, and patient condition. Then it selects an action based on the current context. If the selected action is "FreeWalking," it initiates the FreeWalking behavior. If not, it checks for other possible actions like DevicePairing, BaseMovement, ArmMovement, BatteryCharging, and ConfirmDialog in a fallback manner, executing the first available action. This structure allows the robot to dynamically adapt its behavior based on the environmental and contextual inputs. The tree is designed to ensure that the robot can perform a wide range of tasks while prioritizing critical conditions such as stopping and patient well-being.
output:
<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree" >

    <include path="FreeWalking.xml"/>

    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <ReactiveSequence>
            <CheckStop/>
            <CheckBattery/>
            <CheckDiagnosticStatus/>
            <CheckPatientCondition/>
            <Sequence>
                <Action ID="SkillSelection" selected_action="{selected_action}" />
                <Fallback>
                    <Sequence>
                        <CheckBlackboard input="{selected_action}" compare_to="FreeWalking"/>
                        <FreeWalking/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence>
                        <CheckBlackboard input="{selected_action}" compare_to="DevicePairing"/>
                        <Action ID="DevicePairing"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence>
                        <CheckBlackboard input="{selected_action}" compare_to="BaseMovement"/>
                        <Action ID="BaseMovement"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence>
                        <CheckBlackboard input="{selected_action}" compare_to="ArmMovement"/>
                        <Action ID="ArmMovement"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence>
                        <CheckBlackboard input="{selected_action}" compare_to="BatteryCharging"/>
                        <Action ID="BatteryCharging"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence>
                        <CheckBlackboard input="{selected_action}" compare_to="ConfirmDialog"/>
                        <Action ID="ConfirmDialog"/>
                    </Sequence>
                </Fallback>
            </Sequence>
        </ReactiveSequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
 </root>

input:
The behavior tree represents a robot's active policy for going home based on its battery level. If the "IsGoHomeOkConditionNode" indicates that it is not okay to go home, the robot will perform the "GoHomeMockNode" action, indicating the need to go home. However, if the condition allows, the robot will proceed with the go-home action, which is yet to be implemented as indicated by the comment.

Overall, the behavior tree serves as a decision-making structure for the robot's go-home policy, ensuring that it responds appropriately based on the battery level and the readiness to return home.
output:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<root main_tree_to_execute = "ActiveGoHomePolicy">
    <BehaviorTree ID="ActiveGoHomePolicy">
        <ReactiveSequence name="ActiveBatteryPolicy_ReactiveSequence">
            <Inverter>
                <IsGoHomeOkConditionNode name="Is GoHome OK ConditionNode"/>
            </Inverter>
            <Action ID="GoHomeMockNode" message="Need to go home!!"/>
            <!-- todo: Implement GoHomeActionNode -->
        </ReactiveSequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

</root>

input:
The behavior tree orchestrates a robot's actions to interact with faces. It starts by obtaining a list of faces, selects the first face from the list, and then directs the robot to gaze at the selected face. The tree utilizes various predefined actions such as getting pose arrays, selecting poses, and gazing at targets.

Additionally, it includes a set of defined actions and conditions that can be used within the behavior tree, including tasks like detecting blocks, speaking, moving the base, and waiting for a specified duration. These actions are designed to enable the robot to perform diverse tasks related to interacting with its environment and communicating with humans.

Overall, the behavior tree represents a sequence of actions for the robot to identify and interact with faces, showcasing a structured approach to social interaction and task execution in a robotic context.
output:
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Sequence>
            <Action topic="faces" ID="GetPoseArray" name="GetFaceList" pose_array="face_array"/>
            <Action pose_stamped="face" position_in_list="0" ID="SelectPose" pose_array="{face_array}"/>
            <Action frame_id="base_footprint" fixation_pose="{face}" action_name="gaze" ID="GazeAtTarget"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="ArmCartesian">
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
            <input_port name="frame_id"/>
            <input_port name="pose"/>
            <input_port name="type"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ArmRoutines">
            <input_port name="gesture"/>
            <input_port name="topic"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="BiggerThan">
            <input_port name="A"/>
            <input_port name="B"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="Charge">
            <input_port name="action"/>
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
        </Action>
        <SubTree ID="ChargeST"/>
        <Action ID="CheckBattery">
            <output_port name="battery_state" type="int"/>
            <output_port name="percentage" type="double"/>
            <input_port name="service_name" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="CheckBool">
            <input_port name="variable"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="CheckCharging">
            <output_port name="charging_state" type="int"/>
            <input_port name="service_name" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="CompareDouble">
            <input_port name="A"/>
            <input_port name="B"/>
            <input_port name="Condition"/>
        </Condition>
        <Condition ID="CompareInt">
            <input_port name="A"/>
            <input_port name="B"/>
            <input_port name="Condition"/>
        </Condition>
        <SubTree ID="Congratulate"/>
        <Action ID="DetectBlocks">
            <output_port name="block_list" type="social_cues::BlockList_<std::allocator<void> >"/>
            <input_port name="block_topic" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="DetectFaces">
            <output_port name="faceListOut"/>
            <input_port name="face_topic"/>
        </Action>
        <SubTree ID="ErrorGameState"/>
        <Action ID="FaceCounter">
            <input_port name="faceListIn"/>
            <input_port name="number_faces"/>
        </Action>
        <SubTree ID="GazeAtFace"/>
        <Action ID="GazeAtTarget">
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
            <input_port name="fixation_pose"/>
            <input_port name="frame_id"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GetInt16">
            <output_port name="number"/>
            <input_port name="topic"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GetPoseArray">
            <output_port name="pose_array"/>
            <input_port name="topic"/>
        </Action>
        <SubTree ID="HelpPrepare"/>
        <Action ID="MoveBase">
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
            <input_port name="frame_id"/>
            <input_port name="goal_pose"/>
        </Action>
        <SubTree ID="ResetVariables"/>
        <SubTree ID="SayHello"/>
        <Action ID="SelectBlock">
            <input_port name="block_list" type="social_cues::BlockList_<std::allocator<void> >"/>
            <output_port name="block_pose" type="geometry_msgs::PoseStamped_<std::allocator<void> >"/>
            <input_port name="position_in_list" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SelectPose">
            <input_port name="pose_array"/>
            <output_port name="pose_stamped"/>
            <input_port name="position_in_list"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="Speak">
            <input_port name="action_name" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="language" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="utterance" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="voice" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="WaitForXSeconds">
            <input_port name="result" default="SUCCESS">Result after specified time: SUCCESS or FAILURE</input_port>
            <input_port name="seconds" default="5">Time in seconds</input_port>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>

input: The behavior tree specifies a continuous loop where a robot is programmed to detect a person using object detection. If a person is detected with a confidence level of at least 85%, the robot will perform a "Detected" speech action. If no person is detected or the confidence level is below 85%, the robot will perform a "Not Detected" speech action. This loop will repeat for 9999 cycles, indicating that the robot will continuously attempt to detect a person and provide corresponding feedback through speech actions.
output:
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
	<BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
		<Repeat num_cycles="9999">
			<Sequence>
				<Fallback>
					<Sequence>
						<ObjectDetectionAction class="person" pose="detected_Pose" min_confidence="0.85" />
						<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Detected" />
					</Sequence>
					<Sequence>
						<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Not Detected" />
					</Sequence>

				</Fallback>
			</Sequence>
		</Repeat>
	</BehaviorTree>
</root>
